title: knautia arvensis subsp. pannonica
intro: Knautia arvensis subsp. pannonica, also known as the field scabious or Hungarian scabious, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Dipsacaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Knautia arvensis subsp. pannonica include soothing sore throats, calming digestive issues, and reducing fever, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Knautia arvensis subsp. pannonica include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
